Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Track E: parseSequencesHeader numSeq characterization — small encoding extraction (#826)

* feat: parseSequencesHeader numSeq characterization — small encoding extraction

Add two theorems for parseSequencesHeader (RFC 8878 §3.1.1.3.2):

- parseSequencesHeader_numSeq_small: when 0 < byte0 < 128, numSeq equals
  byte0 (the 1-byte encoding covering up to 127 sequences)
- parseSequencesHeader_numSeq_zero_iff: for byte0 < 128, numSeq = 0 iff
  byte0 = 0 (complete zero/positive characterization)

Both proofs sorry-free. Uses by_cases + if_pos/if_neg for monadic
unfolding through the parseSequencesHeader do-block guards.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* doc: progress entry for parseSequencesHeader numSeq specs

feat: replace .env config with CLI args, support multiple repos

- `npm run setup -- --repo owner/repo --dir /path` installs cron jobs
  with args baked in; safe to re-run (deduplicates by repo)
- `npm run remove -- --repo owner/repo` tears down cron entries
- CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN comes from shell env; setup saves it to
  ~/.dude_env so cron jobs load it without reading the shell profile
- Per-repo lock namespacing so multiple repos run in parallel
- Per-repo log files (logs/issue-spec-owner-repo.log, etc.)
- Removes src/env.ts, .env.example, and all .env reading logic

commit.message:
feat: NC-Max symbiosis — propose/commit pipelining for knowledge chain

Absorbs Ren Zhang et al.'s NC-Max insights (CKB consensus paper) into
Jarvis Mind Network's knowledge chain. Not adopted — symbioted:

1. Change pre-propagation: changes announce to peers immediately via
   /knowledge-chain/announce, not waiting for 5-min epoch. When epoch
   arrives, peers already have changes cached (eliminates "fresh change"
   bottleneck — NC-Max's core insight).

2. Compact epochs: epochs carry shortids + prefilled fresh changes.
   Receiver reconstructs from local change pool. Epoch propagation
   latency becomes size-independent (compact block analog).

3. Freshness penalty: epochs with high % of un-pre-propagated changes
   get VD penalty in fork resolution. Disincentivizes information
   asymmetry (transaction withholding defense).

4. Anti-withholding warning: received epochs with >50% fresh changes
   logged as potential withholding attempts.

All changes backward-compatible — old epochs without freshness metadata
get penalty=1.0 (no effect). Pre-propagation is best-effort.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix multi-platform display: prefer raw enrichment data over socialPlatforms for card/table icons

The displayPlats logic in both card and table views preferred
creator.socialPlatforms (which gets overwritten by enrichment's
incomplete creator_has flags to just ["instagram"]) over the raw
enrichment data which has the actual platform objects with stats.

Flip priority: use getPlatformStatsFromEnrichment(rawEnrich) as the
primary source for platform icons (reads result.tiktok, result.youtube
etc. directly from the IC API response), fall back to socialPlatforms
only when no enrichment data is cached.

commit.message:
fix: prevent infinite recursion in Cloudflare build

OpenNext internally calls `npm run build` to run Next.js build.
When `build` script was itself the OpenNext command, it caused
infinite recursion. Split into `build` (next build) and `build:cf`
(OpenNext build). Added [build] section to wrangler.toml.

commit.message:
Fix ?join= and ?instructor= QR code URLs not routing to game

The PrepareForPlayWizard generates QR codes with ?join=CODE and
?instructor=CODE URLs, but these params were never handled in routing.
Players scanning the QR code were sent to the landing page instead of
the game.

- App.tsx: Initialize showPlayerLogin when ?join= is present; handle
  ?instructor= in init to open game in instructor mode; clean up URL
  after processing
- PlayerLogin.tsx: Read ?join= as alternative to ?code= for auto-fill
- Access.tsx: Read ?join= as alternative to ?code=/?gameCode=

commit.message:
feat: standalone .puml and .mmd file preview with pan & zoom

Open .puml / .plantuml / .pu and .mmd / .mermaid files directly in a
pan & zoom preview panel — no Markdown wrapper needed.  The same
keyboard shortcut (Cmd+Alt+V / Ctrl+Alt+V) auto-selects based on file
type.  Save as PNG / SVG from standalone previews via right-click.

Also includes pre-release quality fixes:
- Security: validate bgColor and mermaidTheme before HTML injection,
  replace innerHTML with textContent for error messages
- Quality: add retry logic for local-server port allocation (TOCTOU),
  extract shared export handler (export-handler.ts)
- Minor: parameter reassignment fix in exporter, remove unnecessary
  void in extension, add JSDoc to exported functions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: preserve OAuth 2.0 refresh token when storing Twitter credentials

Previously, reconnecting Twitter via OAuth 2.0 would overwrite the
refresh_token field with just the OAuth 1.0a token, discarding the
OAuth 2.0 refresh token. This caused the access token to expire with
no way to refresh it (401 on every post).

Now stores both in combined format: oauth2_refresh_token:oauth1_token:oauth1_secret
Also updates oauth.js to extract OAuth 1.0a creds from the combined format.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix DNS for AWS EC2: add nameservers so ACME cert renewal works

Let's Encrypt renewal and the cert for jl4.legalese.com expired.
